repo_prior_art.cite: standard ExactID ATE EIF baseline; no in-catalogue ExactID duplicate needed

published_prior_art.cite: Hahn1998Econometrica / HiranoImbensRidder2003Econometrica / ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML: canonical unconfounded ATE EIF and AIPW/DML baseline

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2 Conjecture 2: LRO normalization equivalence class via coefficient-space projection; current version adds U_J and full-report Gram defects

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO + Hansen1982Econometrica + Newey1994Econometrica: LRO/GMM aggregation and IF projection algebra are standard, but not this exact ATE DML/ADML certificate

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2 Conjecture 2 already sought finite-support LRO/ADML non-equivalence outside the projection class; current version gives a sharper two-cell witness

published_prior_art.cite: Hansen1982Econometrica + ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO: overidentified GMM weighting can select non-Riesz rows, but the stated CEINR ATE witness is not explicitly published

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the LRO/ADML anchors point to construction formulas, not to a location-level open problem or unresolved frontier saying this ATE report-equivalence classification is missing.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist underweights the closest general IF/projection comparators Newey1994Econometrica and IchimuraNewey2022QE; against those, Xi_J reads as routine finite-dimensional projection/GMM algebra unless strengthened.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: As a flagship support result it gives one finite-support tuple, but not a generic open class of CEINR-admissible overidentified LRO dictionaries where alignment failure persists.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a generic open-class separation theorem: characterize an open set of CEINR-admissible overidentified dictionaries with U_J=S_J=0<T_J, rather than only the two-cell witness.
Anchor the flagship claim to a precise gap in CEINR LRO or AutoDML with section/equation/page text showing that report-equivalence classification is left open.
Add an empirical analogue of Xi_J with a stated estimator and asymptotic null distribution for testing LRO/DML/ADML report equivalence.

recommended_next_step: Revise toward a generic estimator-level frontier with a published open-problem anchor; otherwise downgrade/pivot because the current Gram certificate is field-tier.
